checks.a_analysis: The output contains no text that is not present in the source document. All headings, agenda items, and contact information directly correspond to the source PDF content. AI-generated elements like the table caption and image alt text are within the scope of acceptable changes.
The GICC website URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' was verified against the source and is accurate. No other contact information (phone numbers, emails) exists in the source or output.
The source is a single-page document. All visual sections, including the header, the main agenda table, and the footer with meeting dates and the website URL, are represented in the output.
The main agenda table in the source has 6 numbered agenda item rows plus a header row. The output table contains all 6 data rows and the header row, preserving all information.

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for any repeated text blocks or sections. Each agenda item, header, and the footer information appears exactly once, matching the source document. No duplications were found.

The heading structure follows a logical hierarchy. The main council name and the 'AGENDA' are both treated as H2 level, and the '2020 NC GICC Meeting Dates' section is correctly nested as an H3 level. This correctly organizes the document for navigation.

The table structure is faithfully preserved. It maintains the 3-column layout (TIME, AGENDA ITEM, GICC MEMBER or GUEST). All 6 agenda items are correctly placed into separate rows, and the complex alignment in item 5 (where multiple committees are listed against multiple people) is handled accurately within single cells, maintaining the visual and logical mapping of the original.
The reading order in the output (Logo -> Council Name -> Agenda Title -> Date/Time/Info -> Table -> Meeting Dates -> Website) perfectly mirrors the top-to-bottom flow of the original document.

The sub-list of committee reports within agenda item 5 is preserved within the table cell content. While it is not a separate structured list element, the use of bullet characters and newline separators within the cell accurately conveys the list structure of the source.
The URL for the GICC website (https://it.nc.gov/gicc) is correctly captured in the output exactly as it appears in the source PDF. No links are missing or fabricated.

checks.a_analysis: The overall visual organization of the document is preserved. The HTML correctly identifies the main header, the presence of a logo, the agenda table structure, and the footer information. While there are minor differences in line breaks (e.g., the meeting dates at the bottom are on one line instead of two), the general flow and placement of information match the source PDF's intent.

The agenda table is visually corrupted in row 5 ('Committee Reports'). In the source PDF, this row contains two distinct lists that are visually aligned so that each committee report corresponds to a specific person on the right. In the HTML output, the line breaks and alignment are lost; all committee names are lumped into one text block in the center column, and all individuals' names are clumped into a single string of text in the right column ('Paul Badr Debbie Brannan Scott Lokken...'). This breaks the visual association between the committees and their presenters, making the table data difficult to interpret correctly.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1, Agenda Item 5: Shows a list of bulleted committee reports on the left aligned horizontally with corresponding names on the right (e.g., 'Federal Interagency' aligns with 'Scott Lokken').
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table row 5 shows the names as a single continuous block of text without line breaks: 'Paul Badr Debbie Brannan Scott Lokken Dean Grantham John Farley Alex Rankin'.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful image from the source PDF (the NCGICC logo) is correctly present and rendered at the top of the HTML document.
There are no instances of content blocks being repeated or duplicated in the rendered HTML output.
